Scenario.title: Indian Critical Infrastructure Communication Disruption
Scenario.overview: Communications Industry Complex A sophisticated multi-stage attack targeting communication infrastructure leverages AI-driven reconnaissance, custom malware development, and network-level disruption techniques to compromise critical systems.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT), Process, Network Traffic, OS Process, Domain Name Registrar, Malware Artifact, Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Process Creation (Mechanism), Delivery Method, Exploit TEE Vulnerability, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism), Process Injection, Reg.exe Utility, Pass the Hash, Extra Window Memory Injection, Process Creation (Mechanism), IP ID Sequencing Probe

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attackers deploy AI algorithms to analyze publicly available data about communication networks, identifying vulnerabilities in legacy infrastructure and potential entry points for exploitation.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Maliciously modified deployment tools are used to create custom payloads tailored to communication systems, enabling stealthier execution and evasion of standard security measures.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Spear-phished employees receive malicious attachments disguised as routine communications, granting attackers initial foothold access to internal networks.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-67: String Format Overflow in syslog()
stage.description: Unpatched Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) vulnerabilities are exploited to execute arbitrary code on communication devices, bypassing security controls.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: DNS cache poisoning is used to redirect network traffic to command-and-control servers, establishing persistent access to compromised systems.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Network hardware is disabled via crafted traffic patterns, forcing administrators to manually restore operations while maintaining elevated access to critical systems.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into legitimate processes to hide malicious activity, evading detection by traditional monitoring tools.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Altered deployment tools are used to exfiltrate stored credentials from networked devices, enabling further lateral movement.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Network sniffing tools like Reg.exe are deployed to map communication node connections and identify high-value data pathways.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Stolen credentials are used to authenticate to other systems, allowing the attackers to spread across the network undetected.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-47: Buffer Overflow via Parameter Expansion
stage.description: Data is extracted from memory using injection techniques, avoiding disk-based logging and reducing forensic visibility.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Encrypted tunnels are created within normal process flows to exfiltrate data without triggering network anomaly alerts.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Critical network hardware is rendered inoperable through targeted traffic patterns, causing widespread communication outages and operational paralysis.

Scenario.summary: A coordinated attack leveraging AI, custom malware, and network manipulation techniques disrupts communication infrastructure, demonstrating advanced capabilities in persistence, privilege escalation, and denial-of-service operations.
